facts.title: Kimchi at every meal
facts.fact: Kimchi — fermented vegetables seasoned with chili and garlic — accompanies almost every Korean meal. Families traditionally make big batches in late autumn in an event called gimjang.
facts.note: **김장** (gimjang), the communal kimchi-making, is on UNESCO's intangible heritage list.

facts.title: Rice, soup, and many side dishes
facts.fact: A Korean meal centers on rice and soup with many small shared side dishes called banchan, which are refilled for free at most restaurants.
facts.korean_en: I eat rice and side dishes.
facts.note: **하고** means "and" when joining nouns, and is very common in speech.

facts.title: Grill-it-yourself barbecue
facts.fact: At a Korean barbecue you grill meat at your own table and wrap it in lettuce with garlic, ssamjang, and rice. Meals are social and shared.
facts.note: **삼겹살** (samgyeopsal, pork belly) is the most popular cut.
